name: Words of Jesus
description: The red-letter sayings of Jesus of Nazareth, from the Sermon on the Mount to the parables.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
quotes.notes: The first of the Beatitudes, opening the Sermon on the Mount.

quotes.content: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.
quotes.notes: Second Beatitude from the Sermon on the Mount.

quotes.content: Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.
quotes.notes: Third Beatitude, echoing Psalm 37:11.

quotes.content: Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.
quotes.notes: Fourth Beatitude from the Sermon on the Mount.

quotes.content: Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.
quotes.notes: Fifth Beatitude from the Sermon on the Mount.

quotes.content: Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.
quotes.notes: Sixth Beatitude from the Sermon on the Mount.

quotes.content: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
quotes.notes: Seventh Beatitude from the Sermon on the Mount.

quotes.content: Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.
quotes.notes: From the Sermon on the Mount, addressed to Jesus's followers.

quotes.content: Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.
quotes.notes: Sermon on the Mount, continuing the metaphor of light.

quotes.content: Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you.
quotes.notes: Sermon on the Mount teaching on nonretaliation; final semicolon rendered as a period.

quotes.content: For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.
quotes.notes: Sermon on the Mount, on laying up treasures in heaven.

quotes.content: Ye cannot serve God and mammon.
quotes.notes: Sermon on the Mount; the concluding clause of the saying that no man can serve two masters.

quotes.content: Judge not, that ye be not judged.
quotes.notes: Sermon on the Mount, opening the teaching on judging others.

quotes.content: Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.
quotes.notes: Sermon on the Mount, on persistent prayer; final colon rendered as a period.

quotes.content: Therefore all things whatsoever ye would that men should do to you, do ye even so to them: for this is the law and the prophets.
quotes.notes: The Golden Rule, from the Sermon on the Mount.

quotes.content: Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
quotes.notes: Jesus's invitation to the weary.

quotes.content: For what is a man profited, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
quotes.notes: Spoken to the disciples on the cost of discipleship.

quotes.content: Suffer little children, and forbid them not, to come unto me: for of such is the kingdom of heaven.
quotes.notes: Jesus welcoming children brought to him despite the disciples' objections.

quotes.content: Render therefore unto Caesar the things which are Caesar's; and unto God the things that are God's.
quotes.notes: Jesus's reply to the Pharisees' question about paying tribute.

quotes.content: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
quotes.notes: The second great commandment, named by Jesus when asked which is greatest.

quotes.content: Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.
quotes.notes: Jesus's reply to the tempter in the wilderness, quoting Deuteronomy 8:3.

quotes.content: For all they that take the sword shall perish with the sword.
quotes.notes: Said to the disciple who drew a sword at Jesus's arrest in Gethsemane.

quotes.content: For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.
quotes.notes: From Jesus's teaching to the disciples on gathering and forgiveness.

quotes.content: What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.
quotes.notes: Jesus's teaching on marriage in response to the Pharisees.

quotes.content: The kingdom of God is within you.
quotes.notes: Jesus's reply to the Pharisees on when the kingdom of God should come.

quotes.content: Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.
quotes.notes: Spoken from the cross at the crucifixion.

quotes.content: He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.
quotes.notes: Said to the accusers of the woman taken in adultery.

quotes.content: I am the light of the world: he that followeth me shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.
quotes.notes: One of the 'I am' declarations in John's Gospel.

quotes.content: And ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.
quotes.notes: Spoken to the Jews who believed on him, on abiding in his word.

quotes.content: I am the resurrection, and the life: he that believeth in me, though he were dead, yet shall he live.
quotes.notes: Said to Martha before the raising of Lazarus; closing colon rendered as a period.

quotes.content: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.
quotes.notes: Given to the disciples at the Last Supper.

quotes.content: I am the way, the truth, and the life: no man cometh unto the Father, but by me.
quotes.notes: Jesus's answer to Thomas in the upper room discourse.

quotes.content: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.
quotes.notes: From Jesus's farewell discourse on the vine and the branches.

quotes.content: In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
quotes.notes: Closing words of Jesus's farewell discourse to the disciples.
